title: Pod with Init Container
description:
## Pod with Init Container

Init containers run **before** the main application containers start. Each init container must complete successfully before the next one begins, and all must finish before the main containers are started. They are ideal for setup tasks like seeding config files or waiting for a dependency.

**Your task:**

Create a Pod named `init-demo` with:
- An **init container** named `setup` using `busybox:1.36` that writes `ready` into `/shared/status.txt`
- A **main container** named `app` using `busybox:1.36` with command `sleep 3600`
- Both containers mount an `emptyDir` volume at `/shared`

```bash
# Verify the init container wrote the file:
kubectl exec init-demo -- cat /shared/status.txt
```

hints.title: Pod manifest with initContainers
hints.body: Define `spec.initContainers[]` above `spec.containers[]`. Both containers share the emptyDir volume mounted at /shared.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: init-demo
spec:
  initContainers:
  - name: setup
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sh", "-c", "echo ready > /shared/status.txt"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: shared-vol
      mountPath: /shared
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: shared-vol
      mountPath: /shared
  volumes:
  - name: shared-vol
    emptyDir: {}
EOF

commands.description: Init container 'setup' completed with exit code 0
